title: Subjective Probability: A Judgment of Representativeness

design.note: Each participant answered one problem from a questionnaire of ~24 questions. Different participants in each problem.

phenomenon.name: Representativeness Heuristic
phenomenon.description: People judge probability based on how representative (similar) an event is to its parent population, ignoring statistical principles like sample size and variance
phenomenon.key_prediction: Participants will judge less representative events as less probable even when they are objectively equally or more probable

birth_sequence.bias_indicator: judging BGBBBB as less likely than GBGBBG

program_choice.bias_indicator: choosing Program A (representative) over Program B (correct)

key_considerations: Ensure agents understand probability concepts to test whether they override them
Clear presentation of scenarios is critical
Within-subjects design allows testing consistency of bias
common_pitfalls: Agents may correctly apply statistical reasoning
Response parsing must handle both numerical and categorical responses
